channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: IO Interactive announces layoffs after Xbox pulls out of Project Fantasy
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox

Publisher was Xbox Game Studios, according to leaked FTC documents.

IO Interactive has announced layoffs following the end of its relationship with an external partner involved in its February 2023-announced Project Fantasy. Xbox has confirmed to Bloomberg, following leaks from the FTC v. Microsoft court proceedings, that it is the publishing partner in question.

“We’re not reducing our overall investment in games,” a Microsoft spokesperson told Bloomberg. “We expect to invest about the same in content as we did last year. What’s changing is where we’re investing and the kinds of projects we’re backing,”

Xbox itself is reportedly planning to announce a wave of layoffs starting next week that could result in the closure or spin off of at least five studios, as part of a “reset for a stronger Xbox” hinted at by Xbox CEO Asha Sharma.

Here is the full message from IO Interactive on the layoffs:

Dear gaming community,

For a good while, it has been all positive news from IO Interactive. We remain humbled and honored by the response to our latest outing with a young, unproven Bond. A bold new story and a take on one of the most famous characters in entertainment, which the world has embraced with love.

However, today we have to share some more downbeat news. A relationship with an external partner on our own [intellectual property], Project Fantasy, has come to an end. This means we have to adapt to this new reality and its short-term consequences, including staffing decisions, which is what is happening as we write this update, and we are fully committed to supporting those affected through this challenging transition.

Project Fantasy is a game, a world, and an [intellectual property] that we absolutely love and remain 100 [percent] committed to, now and in the future. This wonderful universe will see the light of day.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/io-interactive-announces-layoffs-after-xbox-pulls-out-of-project-fantasy
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Verge: Microsoft weighing Arkane Studios closure, Marvel’s Blade cancelation
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox

Way of layoffs reported to begin on July 6.

Xbox plans to announce a wave of layoffs leading to studio closures, spinoffs, potential mergers, and canceled games next week, according to a The Verge report citing sources familiar with Xbox parent company Microsoft’s plans.

Microsoft is reportedly weighing the closure of at least five studios, including Marvel’s Blade developer Arkane Studios, which operates under the Bethesda Softworks banner. However, the company is also exploring options to sell the studio. Microsoft also wants to cancel Marvel’s Blade, the report claims. The game was originally planned to launch later in 2026, but slipped to late 2027 and is running over budget.

As reported by Bloomberg last week, Microsoft is also said to be trying to spin off Compulsion Games, Double Fine Productions, and Ninja Theory. GamesBeat also reported this week that Microsoft is seeking a buyer for State of Decay 3 developer Undead Labs.

Compulsion Games is reportedly already convinced the studio will close and has begun telling employees to seek work elsewhere. Ninja Theory employees were also informed earlier this month that Microsoft wants to close or spin off the studio.

The wave of layoffs—planned to begin on July 6, according to The Verge’s sources—could result in at least 1,000 job losses if Microsoft closes studios, or at least 500 before the wider cuts.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/the-verge-microsoft-weighing-arkane-studios-closure-marvels-blade-cancelation
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
